problem.title: Construct Binary Tree from Level Order and Inorder Traversal
problem.description: Given two integer arrays levelorder and inorder where levelorder is the level-order (breadth-first) traversal of a binary tree and inorder is the inorder traversal of the same tree, construct and return the binary tree. Assume that the tree has no duplicate node values. The input is given as two level-order arrays (levelorder and inorder) with -1 representing null. Return the constructed tree as a level-order array. This is a harder variant because level-order does not give a natural root-first structure like preorder/postorder. This allows reconstruction in O(N) time after O(N) preprocessing.
constraints: levelorder and inorder are permutations of each other

solution_approaches.name: Recursive with HashMap + Queue Simulation
solution_approaches.intuition: Use inorder map for O(1) root lookup. Simulate level-order construction using a queue while respecting inorder sequence to decide left/right children. The first node in levelorder is always root.

ladders.title: Overview of Level-Order + Inorder Construction
ladders.instruction: Create inorder index map. Root = levelorder[0]. Use queue to process nodes level by level; for each node, decide left/right child by checking if next level-order value appears in left/right subtree range in inorder.

ladders.title: Build Inorder Index Map
ladders.instruction: Create a hashmap that maps each value in inorder to its index for O(1) subtree range lookup.

ladders.title: Queue-based Level Order Construction
ladders.instruction: Start with root = levelorder[0]. Use queue to process nodes. For each node, check next values in levelorder; assign left/right child only if they belong to the correct inorder range.

ladders.title: Child Assignment Logic
ladders.instruction: For current node, look at next values in levelorder. Assign left child if value is in left inorder range, right child if in right range. Use queue to process level by level.

examples.output: left=9 (in left range), right=20 (in right range)

ladders.title: Handle Tree Input
ladders.instruction: Read levelorder and inorder arrays, build map, use queue-based construction.

ladders.title: Output Level-Order Tree
ladders.instruction: Return the level-order array of the constructed tree.

solution_approaches.name: Recursive with Inorder Range + Level Pointer
solution_approaches.intuition: Use a pointer to traverse level-order array sequentially. Root is first in level-order. Recursively build left and right subtrees by counting how many nodes belong to left/right subtree using inorder ranges.

ladders.title: Overview of Recursive Level + Inorder Build
ladders.instruction: Maintain a level-order index pointer. Root = levelorder[levelIdx++]. Find root position in inorder, count left subtree size, build left then right recursively.

ladders.title: Initialize Level Index Pointer
ladders.instruction: Use a mutable levelIdx starting at 0; increment after each root selection.

ladders.title: Find Root Position in Inorder
ladders.instruction: Use hashmap or linear search in current inorder range to find root position and determine left subtree size.

ladders.title: Recursive Subtree Construction
ladders.instruction: Recursively build left subtree with left size nodes from level-order, then right subtree with remaining nodes.

examples.output: left gets next 1 node, right gets next 2

ladders.title: Handle Tree Input
ladders.instruction: Read levelorder and inorder arrays, use recursive level-pointer build.

ladders.title: Output Level-Order Tree
ladders.instruction: Return the level-order array of the constructed tree.

solution_approaches.name: Iterative Queue + Inorder Range Check
solution_approaches.intuition: Use a queue to process level-order nodes. For each node, look ahead in level-order array and assign children only if they belong to the correct inorder range (using inorder map).

ladders.title: Overview of Iterative Queue Construction
ladders.instruction: Root = levelorder[0]. Use queue to process nodes. For each node, check next values in levelorder; assign left/right if value is in left/right inorder range.

ladders.title: Build Inorder Index Map
ladders.instruction: Create hashmap value → index in inorder for O(1) range checking.

ladders.title: Queue Processing Loop
ladders.instruction: Enqueue root. While queue not empty, dequeue node, look at next level-order values, assign left/right child if value belongs to left/right inorder range of current node.

ladders.title: Range Validation for Children
ladders.instruction: For a node with inorder range [inStart, inEnd], a candidate child value belongs to left if its inorder index is in [inStart, rootIdx-1], to right if in [rootIdx+1, inEnd].

ladders.title: Handle Tree Input
ladders.instruction: Read levelorder and inorder arrays, use queue + range check construction.

ladders.title: Output Level-Order Tree
ladders.instruction: Return the level-order array of the constructed tree.

solution_approaches.name: OOP Recursive Build (Class Method)
solution_approaches.intuition: Encapsulate tree building in TreeNode class with recursive method using level-order pointer and inorder ranges.

ladders.title: Overview of OOP Recursive Construction
ladders.instruction: Define TreeNode class with static build method that uses level-order pointer and inorder range checking.

ladders.title: Initialize TreeNode Class
ladders.instruction: Create TreeNode with val, left, right and static build method with level pointer.

ladders.title: Build Tree in OOP
ladders.instruction: Static build method uses level pointer and inorder ranges to create nodes recursively.

ladders.title: Class Recursive Build Method
ladders.instruction: Recursive build(levelIdx, inStart, inEnd) inside class: root = levelorder[levelIdx++]; find root position, build left then right.

ladders.title: Handle OOP Input
ladders.instruction: Instantiate TreeNode class and call static build method with level pointer.

ladders.title: Output Level-Order Tree
ladders.instruction: Return the level-order representation of the constructed tree.

solution_approaches.name: Optimized Recursive (Early Range Check)
solution_approaches.intuition: Use recursive approach with level-order pointer and inorder ranges; early return when range size is 0 or invalid.

ladders.title: Overview of Optimized Recursive Construction
ladders.instruction: Recursive build with level pointer and inorder range; return null for empty range; root = next level-order node.

ladders.title: Initialize Level Pointer
ladders.instruction: Use a mutable levelIdx starting at 0; increment after each root is taken.

ladders.title: Range Validation & Early Return
ladders.instruction: If inStart > inEnd return null; else take next level-order node as root, find its position in inorder range.

ladders.title: Subtree Size Calculation
ladders.instruction: Left subtree size = rootPos - inStart; right size = inEnd - rootPos. Consume exactly that many nodes from level-order for each subtree.

ladders.title: Handle Tree Input
ladders.instruction: Read levelorder and inorder arrays, use recursive level-pointer build.

ladders.title: Output Level-Order Tree
ladders.instruction: Return the level-order array of the constructed tree.

real_world_applications: Reconstructing trees from BFS + inorder traversal logs
Recovering binary trees from level-order snapshots and inorder sequences
Deserializing tree structures in systems that store level-order dumps
Building trees from breadth-first search results combined with inorder
Tree reconstruction in debugging tools that capture level-order traversal
